name: Trading 212
description: Trading 212 Public API — read account summary, instruments, positions, historical orders, dividends, cash transactions, and exports; optionally manage orders. Uses Trading 212 API key + secret Basic auth. For Invest and Stocks ISA accounts only.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Trading 212 Public API key from the Trading 212 app.

credential_fields.name: api_secret
credential_fields.label: API Secret
credential_fields.description: Trading 212 Public API secret paired with the API key.

credential_fields.name: environment
credential_fields.label: Environment
credential_fields.description: Use live for real money or demo for paper trading.

tools.name: get_account_summary
tools.description: Get account summary including cash balance, invested amount, and portfolio value. Returns values in the primary account currency.

tools.name: list_exchanges
tools.description: List all available exchanges with their trading hours and details.

tools.name: list_instruments
tools.description: List all tradeable instruments (stocks, ETFs) with their tickers, names, currencies, and ISINs. Use ticker values (e.g. AAPL_US_EQ) when placing orders.

tools.name: list_pending_orders
tools.description: Get all currently active (pending) orders that are not yet filled, cancelled, or expired.

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: Get details of a specific pending order by its unique ID.

tools.name: place_market_order
tools.description: Place a market order that executes immediately at the best available price. Use positive quantity to BUY, negative quantity to SELL. Set extendedHours=true to allow execution outside regular trading hours. WARNING: Market orders can be subject to price slippage.

ticker.description: Instrument ticker (e.g. AAPL_US_EQ). Get from list-instruments endpoint.

quantity.description: Number of shares. Positive to BUY, negative to SELL (e.g. -10.5 sells 10.5 shares).

extendedHours.description: Allow execution outside regular trading hours

tools.name: place_limit_order
tools.description: Place a limit order that executes at a specified price or better. Use positive quantity to BUY (fills at limitPrice or lower), negative quantity to SELL (fills at limitPrice or higher).

quantity.description: Number of shares. Positive to BUY, negative to SELL.

limitPrice.description: The limit price for the order

timeValidity.description: DAY = expires at market close, GOOD_TILL_CANCEL = stays active until filled or cancelled

tools.name: place_stop_order
tools.description: Place a stop order that triggers a market order when the stop price is reached. Use positive quantity for buy stop, negative quantity for sell stop (stop-loss). Triggered by the instrument's Last Traded Price (LTP).

quantity.description: Number of shares. Positive to BUY, negative to SELL.

timeValidity.description: DAY = expires at market close, GOOD_TILL_CANCEL = stays active until filled or cancelled

tools.name: place_stop_limit_order
tools.description: Place a stop-limit order combining stop and limit features. When the stop price is reached, a limit order is placed at the limit price. Protects against slippage. Use positive quantity for BUY, negative for SELL.

quantity.description: Number of shares. Positive to BUY, negative to SELL.

stopPrice.description: The stop trigger price (triggers the limit order)

limitPrice.description: The limit price for the order once triggered

timeValidity.description: DAY = expires at market close, GOOD_TILL_CANCEL = stays active until filled or cancelled

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: Cancel a pending order by its ID. Cancellation is not guaranteed if the order is already being filled. A successful response means the cancellation request was accepted.

orderId.description: The unique order ID to cancel

tools.name: get_positions
tools.description: Get all open positions with current quantity, average price, current price, and profit/loss.

tools.name: get_order_history
tools.description: Get historical orders with cursor-based pagination. Returns filled, cancelled, and expired orders. Use nextPagePath from response to get next page.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's nextPagePath

tools.name: get_dividend_history
tools.description: Get historical dividend payments with cursor-based pagination. Use nextPagePath from response to get next page.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's nextPagePath

tools.name: get_transaction_history
tools.description: Get historical cash transactions (deposits, withdrawals, fees, etc.) with cursor-based pagination. Use nextPagePath from response to get next page.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's nextPagePath

tools.name: list_exports
tools.description: List all previously requested CSV data exports with their status and download links.

tools.name: create_export
tools.description: Request a new CSV export of your trading data. The export is processed asynchronously — use list-exports to check status and get download link.

dataIncluded.description: What data to include in the export
